summary: End-to-end testing of YouTube Channel Downloader. Backend: 11/11 pytest tests passed (health, channel fetch valid/invalid/empty, cached channel GET + 404, single download invalid + valid headers/stream, bulk empty + over-limit + valid 2-video ZIP). Frontend: hero + example button populates URL, fetch flow shows loading overlay then ChannelHeader (Fireship) and 348 video cards, search filter narrows to 11 for 'javascript', filter tabs (All=348, Videos=300, Shorts=48) work, Select All -> 'Download ZIP (348)', Clear works, individual checkbox visible, single download button present, reset returns to hero.

integration_issues.issue: Select All allows selecting all 348 items, but backend bulk cap is 30. Frontend does not pre-validate or warn user; clicking 'Download ZIP (348)' would fail with 400 from server. Recommend capping selection to 30 in UI or showing a clear warning + disabling button when count>30.

action_items: (Minor) Enforce 30-video bulk cap in frontend: either cap Select All to first 30 of current filtered list, or show warning + disable bulk button when selected.size > 30 to match backend limit.
(Minor) Consider adding loading/disabled state for the bulk download button while ZIP is being generated server-side to prevent duplicate clicks (long-running yt-dlp downloads).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('shutdown') instead of lifespan handlers — fine for now but will break on Starlette upgrade.

_download_video_sync runs entirely in worker thread per item; bulk download is sequential (one-by-one). For 30 videos this can take a long time and the HTTP client may time out before the ZIP starts streaming. Consider streaming progress or parallelizing with a small worker pool.

/api/download/bulk holds the entire ZIP on disk before streaming and only deletes tmpdir after the iterator finishes; if the client disconnects mid-stream the cleanup still runs (good), but very large ZIPs could fill /tmp. Consider zipstream-ng for on-the-fly streaming.
No rate limiting on /api/channel/fetch and /api/download — easy to abuse with yt-dlp. Add basic IP-level throttling for any public deployment.

extract_hashtags only sees flat-extract description, which is usually empty; hashtags will mostly be parsed from titles. Consider a second-pass extract_info per video for accurate hashtags (with cost tradeoff).
seed_data_creation: MongoDB 'channels' collection populated with @fireship channel cache during test run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 11 pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_youtube_downloader.py pass against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Frontend testids: channel-url-input, fetch-channel-btn, example-mrbeast/veritasium/fireship, loading-overlay, channel-header, channel-name, reset-channel-btn, video-search-input, filter-all/videos/shorts, select-all-btn, clear-selection-btn, bulk-download-btn, video-card-{id}, select-video-{id}, download-single-{id}. Channel @fireship returned 348 items (300 videos + 48 shorts). Bulk download server cap=30 but frontend Select All ignores cap.
rca of the issue: No bugs requiring RCA. Only one UX gap: frontend does not enforce 30-video bulk cap before POSTing to /api/download/bulk; backend correctly returns 400 in that case but the user sees a generic error. Mitigation: in BulkBar.jsx, slice selected to first 30 when invoking onBulkDownload, or disable button + show '30 max' helper text when selected.size > 30.
